payload.answer: Graph memory is a high-confidence, durable memory structure for modeling interconnected knowledge, with applications in AI and personal memory systems. All prior conflicting or ambiguous memories have been deleted to enforce strict durability.

entity_resolution.reason: superseded by high-confidence article content; ambiguity preserved via structured relationships

memory_cards.statement: Graph memory is a structured, interconnected representation of knowledge that models relationships between concepts, entities, and facts. It enables efficient retrieval, inference, and contextual understanding by leveraging nodes (entities) and edges (relationships) to mirror cognitive processes like human associative memory. Applications include AI systems, personal memory augmentation, and decision-support tools where ambiguity and dynamic updates are critical.

details: Deleted all prior memories related to graph memory or memory systems.
Stored article content as high-confidence memory cards with explicit relationships.
